content.text: set up GHA CI in this project. reuse workflows from gha-obsidian. but this one will require a more elaborate testing setup with separate check for linting, unit tests and e2e tests

input.command: echo "=== gha-obsidian structure ==="; find /Users/laughedelic/Code/gha-obsidian -type f -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -not -path '*/.git/*' | sort

/Users/laughedelic/Code/gha-obsidian/README.md

3	on:
4	  workflow_call:
5	    inputs:
6	      node-version:
7	        description: Node.js version to use
8	        type: string
9	        default: "20"
10	
11	jobs:
12	  check:
13	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
14	    steps:
15	      - uses: actions/checkout@v7
16	
17	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
18	        with:
19	          node-version: ${{ inputs.node-version }}
20	          cache: npm

1	name: Release
2	
3	on:
4	  workflow_call:
5	    inputs:
6	      node-version:
7	        description: Node.js version to use
8	        type: string
9	        default: "20"
10	
11	permissions:
12	  contents: write
13	  id-token: write
14	  attestations: write
15	
16	jobs:
17	  release:
18	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
19	    steps:
20	      - uses: actions/checkout@v7
21	
22	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
23	        with:
24	          node-version: ${{ inputs.node-version }}
25	          cache: npm
26	
27	      - run: npm ci
28	
29	      - run: npm run build
30	
31	      - run: npm test --if-present
32	
33	      # Obsidian expects main.js and manifest.json, plus styles.css if the plugin has one
34	      - name: Collect release assets
35	        id: assets
36	        run: |
37	          assets="main.js manifest.json"
38	          if [[ -f styles.css ]]; then
39	            assets="$assets styles.css"
40	          fi
41	          echo "list=$assets" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
42	          {
43	            echo "paths<<EOF"
44	            tr ' ' '\n' <<< "$assets"
45	            echo "EOF"
46	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
47	
48	      - name: Attest build provenance
49	        uses: actions/attest-build-provenance@v4
50	        with:
51	          subject-path: ${{ steps.assets.outputs.paths }}
52	
53	      - name: Create release
54	        env:
55	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
56	        run: |
57	          version="$(jq -r .version manifest.json)"
58	          if gh release view "$version" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
59	            echo "Release $version already exists, skipping."
60	            exit 0
61	          fi
62	          gh release create "$version" \
63	            --target "$GITHUB_SHA" \
64	            --title "$version" \
65	            --generate-notes \
66	            ${{ steps.assets.outputs.list }}
67

1	# gha-obsidian
2	
3	Reusable GitHub Actions workflows for [Obsidian](https://obsidian.md) plugins.
4	One place to maintain CI and release automation; every plugin repo just calls
5	these workflows and picks up improvements automatically.
6	
7	The release flow is designed around the [Obsidian community plugin
8	requirements](https://docs.obsidian.md/Plugins/Releasing/Release+your+plugin+with+GitHub+Actions):
9	a GitHub release tagged with the exact version from `manifest.json` (no `v`
10	prefix), with `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` (if present)
11	attached as individual assets.
12	
13	## Usage
14	
15	Add these two files to your plugin repo.
16	
17	`.github/workflows/ci.yml` — lint, build, and test on every push and PR:
18	
19	```yaml
20	name: CI
21	
22	on:
23	  push:
24	    branches: [main]
25	  pull_request:
26	
27	jobs:
28	  check:
29	    uses: laughedelic/gha-obsidian/.github/workflows/ci.yml@main
30	```
31	
32	`.github/workflows/release.yml` — cut a release whenever the version in
33	`manifest.json` changes on `main`:
34	
35	```yaml
36	name: Release
37	
38	on:
39	  push:
40	    branches: [main]
41	    paths: [manifest.json]
42	
43	permissions:
44	  contents: write     # create the release
45	  id-token: write     # build provenance attestation
46	  attestations: write # build provenance attestation
47	
48	jobs:
49	  release:
50	    uses: laughedelic/gha-obsidian/.github/workflows/release.yml@main
51	```
52	
53	Releasing a new version is then just: bump `version` in `manifest.json`
54	(and `package.json` / `versions.json` as appropriate), merge to `main`, done.
55	The workflow is idempotent — if a release for that version already exists,
56	it skips instead of failing.
57	
58	## What the workflows do
59	
60	### `ci.yml`
61	
62	- `npm ci`
63	- `npm run lint` (skipped if the script doesn't exist)
64	- `npm run build`
65	- `npm test` (skipped if the script doesn't exist)
66	
67	### `release.yml`
68	
69	- `npm ci`, `npm run build`, `npm test` (if present)
70	- Generates [build provenance attestations](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-for-github-actions/using-artifact-attestations)
71	  for the release assets
72	- Creates a GitHub release named after `manifest.json`'s `version`, with
73	  auto-generated notes and the plugin assets attached
74	
75	## Inputs
76	
77	Both workflows accept:
78	
79	| Input          | Default | Description            |
80	| -------------- | ------- | ---------------------- |
81	| `node-version` | `"20"`  | Node.js version to use |
82	
83	```yaml
84	jobs:
85	  check:
86	    uses: laughedelic/gha-obsidian/.github/workflows/ci.yml@main
87	    with:
88	      node-version: "22"
89	```
90	
91	## Assumptions
92	
93	- npm with a committed `package-lock.json` (`npm ci`)
94	- `npm run build` produces `main.js` at the repo root
95	- `manifest.json` at the repo root is the source of truth for the version
96	
97	## Versioning
98	
99	Plugin repos reference `@main` to always get the latest workflow. If you
100	prefer stability over automatic updates, pin to a tag or commit SHA instead:
101	
102	```yaml
103	uses: laughedelic/gha-obsidian/.github/workflows/release.yml@v1
104	```
105

2	  "name": "obsidian-true-outliner",

5	  "main": "src/index.ts",

7	    "doc": "docs"

17	    "vault:reset": "git checkout -- test-vault",
18	    "test:e2e": "npm run build:plugin && wdio run e2e/wdio.conf.mts",

input.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:

env:
  node-version: "20"

jobs:
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.node-version }}
          cache: npm

      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run lint
      - run: npm run build
      - run: npm run build:e2e

  unit-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.node-version }}
          cache: npm

      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm test

  e2e-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.node-version }}
          cache: npm

      - run: npm ci

      # Caches the downloaded Obsidian installer(s) used by wdio-obsidian-service
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: .obsidian-cache
          key: obsidian-cache-${{ runner.os }}-earliest-latest

      - run: npm run build:plugin

      # Obsidian is an Electron app and needs a display even "headless" in CI
      - run: xvfb-run --auto-servernum npm run test:e2e

input.content:
name: Release

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths: [manifest.json]

permissions:
  contents: write     # create the release
  id-token: write     # build provenance attestation
  attestations: write # build provenance attestation

jobs:

The file /Users/laughedelic/Code/obsidian-true-outliner/.github/workflows/release.yml has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text:
Setup is complete and locally verified. Summary:

**`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** — custom (not reused as-is, since `gha-obsidian`'s `ci.yml` bundles everything into one job) with three independent jobs as requested:
- `lint` — eslint + `tsc --noEmit` typecheck for both `src` and `e2e`
- `unit-test` — `vitest run`
- `e2e-test` — builds the plugin, caches the downloaded Obsidian binary (`.obsidian-cache`), runs wdio e2e tests under `xvfb-run` (Obsidian is Electron and needs a display even in CI)

**`.github/workflows/release.yml`** — reuses `gha-obsidian`'s `release.yml` directly via `workflow_call`, since that one didn't need changes.

**Found and fixed a real blocker along the way**: `npm ci` was failing on a peer-dependency conflict (`obsidian@1.13.1` peer-requires `@codemirror/state@6.5.0` exactly, but the repo pulls in `^6.7.1`). Added [.npmrc](.npmrc) with `legacy-peer-deps=true` — this is what let your local `node_modules` diverge from the lockfile silently. Also set `node-version: "26"` in both workflows to match your local dev environment (`@types/node ^26.1.1`, `node --version` → v26.5.0) instead of `gha-obsidian`'s default of 20.

I verified `npm ci`, lint, `build`, `build:e2e`, and unit tests all pass cleanly end-to-end locally. I did not run the actual e2e suite (it downloads and launches a real Obsidian instance — heavy for a local sanity check), so that's worth watching on the first CI run.

## Overview

Create a pull request for the changes in this session.

## Steps

1. Check for uncommitted changes. If any exist, stage and commit them with a message that describes what changed and why.
2. Push the branch to the remote.
3. Open a PR with `gh pr create --draft`. Write a title that summarizes the change and a body that covers what changed, why, and anything a reviewer should know. If the repo has a PR template, follow it.
4. Report the PR URL when done. Wrap the URL in a `<pr-created>` tag on its own line so the UI can render a live status card, like this: `<pr-created>https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123</pr-created>`

If a PR already exists for this branch, push any new commits and report the existing URL wrapped in the same `<pr-created>` tag instead of creating a duplicate.

## Target

Repository: laughedelic/obsidian-true-outliner
Branch: main
Working directory: /Users/laughedelic/Code/obsidian-true-outliner
</create-pr-command>

breaks `npm ci` otherwise.

input.command:
gh pr create --draft --title "ci: add GitHub Actions workflows (lint, unit tests, e2e tests)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Adds `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with three independent jobs — `lint`, `unit-test`, `e2e-test` — rather than reusing `laughedelic/gha-obsidian`'s `ci.yml` as-is, since that reusable workflow bundles lint/build/test into a single job and this repo wants them reported as separate checks.
- `e2e-test` builds the plugin, caches the downloaded Obsidian binary (`.obsidian-cache`), and runs the wdio e2e suite under `xvfb-run` (Obsidian is Electron and needs a display even "headless" in CI).
- Adds `.github/workflows/release.yml`, reusing `laughedelic/gha-obsidian`'s `release.yml` directly via `workflow_call` — this one didn't need any customization.
- Adds `.npmrc` with `legacy-peer-deps=true`: without it, `npm ci` fails because `obsidian@1.13.1`'s peer dependency on `@codemirror/state@6.5.0` conflicts with this repo's `^6.7.1` devDependency. This was silently masked locally by a stale `node_modules`.
- Both workflows pin `node-version: "26"` to match the local dev environment (`@types/node ^26.1.1`, actual `node --version` → v26.5.0) instead of `gha-obsidian`'s default of 20.

## Test plan
- [x] `npm ci` (with `.npmrc` fix) installs cleanly
- [x] `npm run lint` passes
- [x] `npm run build` (typecheck) passes
- [x] `npm run build:e2e` (typecheck for e2e) passes
- [x] `npm test` (vitest) passes — 190/190
- [ ] `e2e-test` job — not run locally (spins up a real Obsidian instance); verify on first CI run

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"
